channel.title: Daily Briefing
channel.description: A daily briefing on weather, tech, health, business, theology, philosophy, and clear thinking.

item.title: Daily Briefing — 2026-03-26
item.description:
Weather

A cool, showery Thursday in the valley. Environment Canada calls for a high of 10°C with a 30% chance of showers and a hard overnight drop to –1°C — genuine frost risk, so cover anything tender. The Coquihalla is getting hammered: a snowfall warning has 25–35 cm piling up between Hope and Merritt, easing by midday. Friday warms to 12°C with a mix of sun and cloud; Saturday 13°C but overcast; Sunday pulls back to 11°C. Monday looks like 14°C with a 60% chance of showers returning. Trails are drying slowly from last week's atmospheric river — expect mud, not flow.

Tech

Claude Code

Anthropic published a detailed engineering blog post on Claude Code's new auto mode — a middle ground between clicking "approve" on every permission prompt and the nuclear --dangerously-skip-permissions flag. The motivation: users approve 93% of prompts, breeding approval fatigue. Auto mode uses two classifier layers: a server-side prompt-injection probe on tool outputs, and a Sonnet 4.6 transcript classifier that evaluates each action before execution. The classifier is deliberately reasoning-blind — it sees only user messages and tool calls, not Claude's internal chain-of-thought. Enable it with claude --enable-auto-mode and cycle with Shift+Tab; available now as a research preview on Team plans, with Enterprise and API rolling out shortly.

Competitors

OpenAI's legacy Deep Research mode was removed today as previously announced — the current deep research experience is unaffected and historical conversations remain accessible. Separately, OpenAI published guidance on helping developers build safer AI experiences for teens, and ChatGPT Business now supports write actions for Google and Microsoft apps — drafting emails in Outlook, creating Docs and Sheets, and scheduling via calendar. Write actions are disabled by default until workspace admins opt in.

Apple

iOS 26.4 shipped with fixes for 36 security vulnerabilities on iPhone, plus CarPlay's new Ambient Music widget and voice-based chatbot app support. AirPods Max 2 pre-orders opened yesterday [previously announced] in five colours with the H2 chip delivering 1.5× better ANC, lossless USB-C audio, and Live Translation — same $549 price point, shipping early April.

Business

Markets

S&P 500 closed at 6,592 (+0.5%), snapping a losing streak. TSX rallied 1.4% to 32,383. The big story is oil: Brent crashed 5.9% to $98.28 and WTI fell 5.1% to $87.68 on rising ceasefire expectations — the sharpest single-day drop since the Iran war began. VIX remains elevated at 27.4.

Canada Macro

Senior Deputy Governor Carolyn Rogers delivered a notable speech today warning that the next five years could be as economically tumultuous as the last five. She identified three structural forces — US trade protectionism, reduced immigration, and AI adoption — as permanent shifts the BoC must navigate. The USMCA renewal this year adds further uncertainty. CAD trading at 72.2 cents US.

US Macro

The oil crash is doing the Fed's work for it — if ceasefire prospects hold, the energy-driven inflation scare that rewrote rate expectations three weeks ago could partially unwind. Equities rallied on the prospect, though the VIX at 27 suggests the market is far from convinced the war is over. Durable goods data due today.

Psalm of the Day — Psalm 4

1 Answer me when I call, O God of my righteousness! You have given me relief when I was in distress. Be gracious to me and hear my prayer! 2 O men, how long shall my honor be turned into shame? How long will you love vain words and seek after lies? Selah 3 But know that the LORD has set apart the godly for himself; the LORD hears when I call to him. 4 Be angry, and do not sin; ponder in your own hearts on your beds, and be silent. Selah 5 Offer right sacrifices, and put your trust in the LORD. 6 There are many who say, "Who will show us some good? Lift up the light of your face upon us, O LORD!" 7 You have put more joy in my heart than they have when their grain and wine abound. 8 In peace I will both lie down and sleep; for you alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.

Scripture quotations are from the ESV® Bible (The Holy Bible, English Standard Version®), © 2001 by Crossway, a publishing ministry of Good News Publishers. Used by permission. All rights reserved.

Exegesis

Psalm 4 is an evening prayer of trust — a companion piece to Psalm 3's morning confidence. The opening appeal to "the God of my righteousness" invokes covenantal standing. Verse 4's command — "Be angry, and do not sin" — is notoriously difficult: the imperative can mean "tremble" or "be agitated" as much as "be angry." The chiastic structure pivots on verse 5's call to trust. The theological climax is verse 7: joy that exceeds material abundance — grain and wine being the Deuteronomic markers of covenant blessing.

SEP — Personal Identity

plato.stanford.edu/entries/personal-identity/

The problem of personal identity asks what makes a person at one time the same person at a later time. The dominant positions divide into psychological continuity theories and biological/animalist theories. Locke's prince-cobbler thought experiment drives psychological accounts, but faces the reduplication problem. Parfit argued that identity is not what matters — psychological continuity and connectedness are. Animalists counter that we were once fetuses with no psychological life. The debate has practical stakes: advance directives, criminal responsibility, and digital uploads.

Cognitive Bias — Sunk Cost Fallacy

The sunk cost fallacy is your tendency to continue investing in something because of what you've already spent, rather than what you stand to gain going forward. You sit through the last hour of a terrible film because you paid $15 for the ticket, as if leaving would un-spend the money. Munger called it "the shoe that doesn't fit" — the rational move is always to ask what the next dollar buys, not what the last dollar bought.

item.title: Daily Briefing — 2026-03-22
item.description:
Weather

Cool and unsettled as the tail end of last week's atmospheric river wrings itself out. Environment Canada calls for a high of 6°C today with a 60% chance of scattered showers. Overnight low drops to –1°C — expect patchy frost by morning. Monday offers a brief reprieve with a high near 10°C and mostly dry skies, but Tuesday brings the next system: 28 mm of rain forecast with gusty winds up to 55 km/h. Layer up and plan outdoor work for Monday's window.

Tech

Claude Platform / Models

Anthropic has temporarily doubled usage limits across Claude.ai, Cowork, and Claude Code through the end of Friday, March 27. The bonus applies all day on weekends and on weekdays outside peak hours (before 5 AM or after 11 AM PT) — bonus usage does not count against your weekly limit. Separately, there was a brief outage on Saturday with elevated error rates on Claude Opus and Sonnet 4.6 around 01:00 UTC, resolved within the hour.

Competitors

OpenAI announced that legacy Deep Research mode will be removed on Thursday, March 26 — the current deep research experience is unaffected and historical conversations remain accessible. The Astral acquisition [previously noted] has not yet closed; community anxiety about uv's future under OpenAI continues to simmer.

Apple

Quiet weekend. Apple TV turned 19 on Saturday. iOS 26.4 public release is expected this week.

General Interest

The Trivy GitHub Actions supply chain compromise escalated into a self-propagating npm worm dubbed CanisterWorm, spreading across 47 npm packages. Over 10,000 GitHub workflow files reference the compromised trivy-action — 75 of 76 version tags affected. Pin to a verified commit hash immediately.

Business

Markets

Weekend — no new trading data since Friday's close. S&P 500 finished at 6,506 (–1.5%), Dow 45,577 (–1.0%), Nasdaq 21,648 (–2.0%). Brent settled at $112.19, up 3.3% on Friday. The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.38%.

Canada Macro

Quiet day. Next Bank of Canada rate decision April 29. Money markets continue to price 75 bps of hikes by year-end, though economists warn that hiking into 6.7% unemployment and negative Q4 GDP would compound household pain. CAD steady around 72.87 cents US.

US Macro

No new data over the weekend. The week ahead brings existing home sales (Tuesday) and durable goods orders (Wednesday). The Fed's message from Wednesday's press conference is still being digested: hold at 3.50–3.75%, core PCE revised up to 2.7%, lone dissent from Miran favoring a cut.

Psalm of the Day — Psalm 3

A Psalm of David, when he fled from Absalom his son.

1O LORD, how many are my foes! Many are rising against me; 2many are saying of my soul, "There is no salvation for him in God." Selah 3But you, O LORD, are a shield about me, my glory, and the lifter of my head. 4I cried aloud to the LORD, and he answered me from his holy hill. Selah 5I lay down and slept; I woke again, for the LORD sustained me. 6I will not be afraid of many thousands of people who have set themselves against me all around. 7Arise, O LORD! Save me, O my God! For you strike all my enemies on the cheek; you break the teeth of the wicked. 8Salvation belongs to the LORD; your blessing be on your people! Selah

Scripture quotations are from the ESV® Bible (The Holy Bible, English Standard Version®), © 2001 by Crossway, a publishing ministry of Good News Publishers. Used by permission. All rights reserved.

Exegesis

Psalm 3 is the first individual lament in the Psalter with a historical superscription linking it to David's flight from Absalom (2 Samuel 15–18). The psalm traces a movement from crisis through trust to serene confidence. The taunt in verse 2 — "there is no yešûʿâ for him in God" — is directly refuted by the closing verse: "Salvation belongs to the LORD," forming a theological inclusio. The verse 5 confession — "I lay down and slept; I woke again" — is sharper against the Absalom narrative: to sleep when thousands surround you is either foolishness or faith.

SEP — The Problem of Induction

plato.stanford.edu/entries/induction-problem/

The problem of induction asks what justifies inference from observed cases to unobserved ones. Hume showed that inductive justification is circular and no deductive argument bridges past to future. Popper accepted this and proposed falsificationism; Reichenbach offered a pragmatic vindication; Goodman deepened the problem with the "grue" paradox. Bayesian approaches formalize updating but leave the choice of priors contested. The problem has never been solved in a way that commands consensus.

Cognitive Bias — Confirmation Bias

Confirmation bias is your tendency to seek out, interpret, and remember information that confirms what you already believe — while discounting contradictory evidence. Peter Wason's 2-4-6 task showed subjects almost universally test only examples consistent with their hypothesis, never trying to falsify it. The corrective: actively seek the strongest version of the opposing case before concluding you are right.

item.title: Daily Briefing — 2026-03-21
item.description:
Weather

The atmospheric river has finally moved on, leaving the Fraser Valley waterlogged but drying. Environment Canada is calling for a high near 9°C today with a 40% chance of scattered post-frontal showers. Freezing levels dropping to 500–1,000 metres; overnight low could touch 0°C — first frost risk this week. Sunday will be the cold one, high only 6°C, low near –1°C. Monday warms to 9°C but rain returns. Trails saturated and river levels elevated through the weekend.

Tech

Competitors

OpenAI announced it is acquiring Astral, the company behind uv (package manager), Ruff (linter), and ty (type checker). The Astral team will join Codex, now claiming more than two million users. OpenAI says it will continue supporting the open-source projects, but the community is anxious: uv had 126 million PyPI downloads last month. Deal not yet closed.

Apple

Apple introduced AirPods Max 2 with H2 chip, 1.5× better ANC, Live Translation, and Camera Remote via Digital Crown. Same $549; pre-orders March 25. iOS 26.4 RC features Apple Music Concerts, AI Playlist Playground, eight new emoji, RCS encryption testing, Stolen Device Detection on by default. Public release expected next week.

Business

Markets

Weekend summary. The story is the speed at which rate expectations have inverted. VIX jumped 11% to 26.78. Treasury yields surged 12–15 bps Friday after reports of three more warships deploying to the Gulf. 10-year at 4.38%, up 41 bps since the war began.

Canada Macro

Money markets now pricing 75 bps of BoC rate hikes by year-end, with >20% chance of a hike at April 29. Economists warn hiking into weakness could compound household pain. CAD flat at 72.81 cents US.

US Macro

Bond traders assign a 50% probability of a Fed rate hike by October. Money markets see no chance of any cut in 2026. Three weeks ago, two cuts were fully priced in; the oil shock has rewritten the rate trajectory.

Psalm of the Day — Psalm 2

1Why do the nations rage and the peoples plot in vain? 2The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the LORD and against his Anointed, saying, 3"Let us burst their bonds apart and cast away their cords from us." 4He who sits in the heavens laughs; the Lord holds them in derision. 5Then he will speak to them in his wrath, and terrify them in his fury, saying, 6"As for me, I have set my King on Zion, my holy hill." 7I will tell of the decree: The LORD said to me, "You are my Son; today I have begotten you. 8Ask of me, and I will make the nations your heritage, and the ends of the earth your possession. 9You shall break them with a rod of iron and dash them in pieces like a potter's vessel." 10Now therefore, O kings, be wise; be warned, O rulers of the earth. 11Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice with trembling. 12Kiss the Son, lest he be angry, and you perish in the way, for his wrath is quickly kindled. Blessed are all who take refuge in him.

Scripture quotations are from the ESV® Bible (The Holy Bible, English Standard Version®), © 2001 by Crossway, a publishing ministry of Good News Publishers. Used by permission. All rights reserved.

Exegesis

Psalm 2 is a royal psalm — almost certainly composed for the enthronement of a Davidic king. The four stanzas progress through four voices: the narrator's incredulous question (vv. 1–3), YHWH's derisive response from heaven (vv. 4–6), the king's recitation of the divine decree (vv. 7–9), and a wisdom teacher's admonition (vv. 10–12). The decree formula — "You are my Son; today I have begotten you" — employs ANE adoption language: the king becomes YHWH's son by covenantal appointment, not ontological generation. The verb rāʿaʿ in v. 9 admits an ambiguity: "break" or "shepherd," which the LXX exploited. The closing ʾašrê forms an inclusio with Psalm 1:1, binding both psalms as a single prologue to the Psalter.

SEP — Underdetermination of Scientific Theory

plato.stanford.edu/entries/scientific-underdetermination/

The underdetermination thesis holds that evidence may be insufficient to determine which of several rival theories is correct. Holist underdetermination (Duhem-Quine) says hypotheses are never tested in isolation. Contrastive underdetermination claims empirically equivalent rivals can always be constructed. Laudan and Leplin argue genuine empirical equivalence is transient. The debate cuts to the heart of scientific realism. The insight generalizes: in any domain, data rarely compel a single conclusion, and tie-breaking criteria may be pragmatic rather than truth-tracking.

Cognitive Bias — Availability Heuristic

The availability heuristic is your tendency to judge probability by how easily examples come to mind — not by actual frequency. Kahneman and Tversky showed people believe more words start with "r" than have "r" third — because starting letters are easier to retrieve. The corrective: consult the base rate before trusting your gut.

item.title: Daily Briefing — 2026-03-20
item.description:
Weather

The atmospheric river that has battered the Fraser Valley all week is finally losing its grip. Environment Canada still has a rainfall warning in effect for the central Fraser Valley, with another 10–20 mm expected before showers taper near midday. High near 11°C with southwest gusts to 50 km/h; overnight low around 4°C. The FVRD declared a state of emergency yesterday for Electoral Area E in the Chilliwack River Valley, and roughly 30 properties along the Chilliwack River remain under evacuation alert. A mudslide in Coquitlam forced a couple to be airlifted on Thursday. Saturday: 10°C with a 40% chance of showers. Sunday drier at 11°C. Trails saturated through the weekend.

Tech

Claude Code

Two releases in two days. Claude Code v2.1.81 shipped today with a --bare flag for scripted -p calls — skips hooks, LSP, plugin sync, and skill directory walks. Also: --channels permission relay, OAuth token refresh fixes, and MCP tool call output that collapses into a single line. Yesterday's v2.1.80 added rate_limits for statusline scripts, inline plugin declarations via source: 'settings', effort frontmatter for skills, and fixed --resume dropping parallel tool results.

Claude Platform

Anthropic published "What 81,000 People Want from AI," a qualitative study spanning 159 countries — the largest of its kind. Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia substantially more optimistic than the West; independent workers reported three times the rate of economic empowerment. The Pentagon filed new declarations arguing Anthropic's employment of foreign nationals poses security risks, as part of its motion to dismiss the lawsuit. Jack Clark briefed House Homeland Security behind closed doors. Hearing on temporary relief set for March 24.

Competitors

OpenAI released GPT-5.4 Mini and Nano on March 17, now rolling out to ChatGPT Free and Go users. GPT-5.4 Mini approaches full GPT-5.4 on SWE-Bench Pro while running 2× faster. Gemini got a Workspace update — contextual drafting in Docs, AI spreadsheets in Sheets, deck generation in Slides, and cross-app intelligence.

Apple

Tim Cook: "best launch week ever for first-time Mac customers." All eight Neo configurations sold out online — delivery estimates pushed to April 6–13. New iPhone Fold patents show iPad-like multitasking layout.

General Interest

Super Micro Computer plunged 33% after co-founder Wally Liaw charged with conspiring to smuggle $2.5 billion in Nvidia GPU servers to China. Supermicro not named as defendant. Nvidia dropped 1.7%; Dell rose 6%.

Business

Markets

Third consecutive daily decline, fourth straight week in the red. S&P 500 fell 1.5% to 6,507, Nasdaq dropped 2.0% to 21,654, Dow shed 0.96% to 45,577. Russell 2000 in correction territory. Brent settled at $112.19/bbl (+3.3%), 10-year yield jumped to 4.38%, gold hit $4,667. Planet Labs surged 25% on breakeven Q4 and 41% revenue growth.

Canada Macro

Bank of Canada held at 2.25% Wednesday. CPI eased to 1.8% but BoC warned Middle East war will boost energy prices. Unemployment at 6.7%. Next: April 29.

US Macro

Fed held at 3.50–3.75%, 11-1 vote (Miran dissented). GDP growth revised to 0.9%, PCE raised to 2.7%. Job creation near zero, unemployment 4.4%. Seven of 19 FOMC officials expect no cuts this year.

Psalm of the Day — Psalm 1

1Blessed is the man who walks not in the counsel of the wicked, nor stands in the way of sinners, nor sits in the seat of scoffers; 2but his delight is in the law of the LORD, and on his law he meditates day and night. 3He is like a tree planted by streams of water that yields its fruit in its season, and its leaf does not wither. In all that he does, he prospers. 4The wicked are not so, but are like chaff that the wind drives away. 5Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous; 6for the LORD knows the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked will perish.

Scripture quotations are from the ESV® Bible (The Holy Bible, English Standard Version®), © 2001 by Crossway, a publishing ministry of Good News Publishers. Used by permission. All rights reserved.

Exegesis

Psalm 1 is a Torah psalm — a wisdom composition placed at the gateway of the Psalter to frame everything that follows as meditation on divine instruction. The architecture is starkly binary: two ways, two images. The righteous are a tree (ʿēṣ), planted and fruitful; the wicked are chaff (môṣ), weightless and wind-scattered. The opening ʾašrê is a declaration, not a prayer. The three verbs — walks, stands, sits — trace descent from casual association to settled identification. The Hebrew tôrâ carries the broader sense of "instruction." The tree image draws on ANE royal iconography but democratizes it. The verb šātal ("planted") implies deliberate placement by another agent. The closing yādaʿ ("knows") operates in its covenantal register: intimate, committed relationship. The psalm presents no third way.

SEP — Formal Epistemology

plato.stanford.edu/entries/formal-epistemology/

Formal epistemology applies mathematical tools — probability theory, decision theory, modal logic — to traditional epistemological questions. Bayesian epistemology models belief as degrees of credence and updates via conditionalization. Accuracy-first epistemology (Joyce, Pettigrew) derives probabilism from the norm that credences should minimize distance from truth. The field also covers belief revision (AGM framework), epistemic logic, and epistemic game theory. Critics argue formalization purchases precision at the cost of depth; defenders reply that informal epistemology has spent centuries circling the same problems. The field embodies "the hope that reason can be productively turned upon itself."

Cognitive Bias — Anchoring

Anchoring is the tendency to rely too heavily on the first piece of information you encounter, even when it is arbitrary. Tversky and Kahneman demonstrated this with a rigged wheel of fortune — those who saw 65 estimated higher than those who saw 10, despite the number being random. The same mechanism operates in salary negotiations, real estate, and courtroom sentencing. Expertise provides no immunity. The corrective: generate your own independent estimate before responding to any initial figure.
